items.title: At least 32 dead after major earthquake strikes southern Philippines
items.summary: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.

items.title: Rare footage captured of Great White shark in Mediterranean Sea
items.summary: A volunteer diver has described shaking as he filmed his encounter with an endangered Great White shark between Tunisia and Sicily.

items.title: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure
items.summary: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract Party secures nearly 50% of the vote, comfortably beating the other contenders.

items.title: Knicks' long-awaited NBA run electrifies NYC, with Trump set to attend key game
items.summary: The Knicks' stunning winning streak has energised the city as the New York basketball team prepares for the third game of the finals series at its home arena.

items.title: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks
items.summary: The Ukrainian president's meeting with his staunchest European allies came as US President Donald Trump's focus shifts to the war with Iran.

items.title: Trump abruptly ends NBC interview after clash over 'rigged election' claim
items.summary: During the interview, the president was repeatedly challenged on several points by the show's presenter Kristen Welker.

items.title: 'A World Cup for them not us': Fans' anger at US travel bans and visa restrictions
items.summary: Fans across the world say US travel bans and visa regulations make them feel excluded from the World Cup.

items.title: Malawians repatriated from South Africa amid xenophobia concerns
items.summary: Malawi is among several African nations transporting their citizens out of the country following reports of violence against migrants.

items.title: Stock markets fall on tech plunge and renewed Middle East attacks
items.summary: Markets in Europe and Asia were hit by a tech sell-off, and oil rises as Iran and Israel launch attacks at each other

items.title: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
items.summary: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

items.title: Hundreds of captives freed from Boko Haram mountain hideout
items.summary: The group includes many women and children who were abducted in March from an area close to Cameroon.

items.title: Mexico City attempts record-breaking wave
items.summary: As part of the countdown to the World Cup, Mexico City is attempting to surpass the world record for largest wave.

items.title: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
items.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

items.title: Eriksen conscious after collapsing in Denmark game
items.summary: Christian Eriksen collapsed during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine but is now conscious, says the Danish Football Association.

items.title: Friendship or leverage: Why is Xi Jinping in North Korea?
items.summary: Beijing is trying to reassert influence over a strategically vital yet deeply unpredictable partner.

items.title: 'We don't look at the sky anymore': The Air India crash victims who were not on the plane
items.summary: A grandfather, a survivor, a witness: one year after the crash, the people on the ground tell their stories.

items.title: Spain's visitor numbers hit new highs as tourists avoid Middle East
items.summary: The European country had 9.1 million international visitors in April, the most ever for that month.

items.title: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
items.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

items.title: Steve Rosenberg: Russia's economic forum overshadowed by drone attacks on St Petersburg
items.summary: The BBC's Russia editor saw Putin's flagship economic event overshadowed by Ukrainian drones attacks.

items.title: Mexicans chase a world record wave - but is the trend even Mexican?
items.summary: Thousands lined the streets in Mexico City on Saturday as they attempted to set the world record for largest human wave.

items.title: Rumours and speculation as fans forecast date of Taylor Swift's wedding
items.summary: Swifties worldwide are looking for clues about the superstar's nuptials to American football star Travis Kelce.

items.summary: A volunteer diver has described shaking as he filmed his encounter with an endangered Great White shark between Tunisia and Sicily.

items.title: Is there an AI stock market bubble, and is it ready to burst?
items.summary: Despite the Iran war, inflation and debt fears, US markets keep hitting record highs, fueled largely by AI. BBC's Samira Hussain looks into whether that bubble will burst.

items.title: Why are devastating mice plagues happening in Australia?
items.summary: Crops are being ravaged and homes are being invaded by the rodents - and it’s costing farmers hundreds of thousands of dollars.

items.title: 'Extremely intelligent' bear on the run in Japan after injuring four people
items.summary: Authorities said that a bear in north-east Japan has eluded capture by opening a window.
